nodes.text: So the Exchange found us another company willing to brave the old quarters. Good. Aldenmere was a hundred lamplit streets once — we hold six. Take the Council's first reclamation commission: secure the Old Docks, then follow the gold marker back to this hall for your reward.

The squatter gangs hold three yards along the waterfront. Break them all and the docks are ours again. Then follow the gold turn-in marker back to Council Hall. Watch for the rats — they hunt in packs.
The lamps are lit on the waterfront tonight — first time in nine years. The Council is in your debt. Rest, resupply... then we must speak of the Market. The dead walk there.

You gave the drowned their rest. But before we dare the Temple, there is uglier work: an orc warband has dug into the sunken quarter south of the walls, and the Council will not assault the Temple with Splitjaw's raiders at its back.

Splitjaw holds the old drainage quarter with two picket bands and his war-tent. Break all three and bring me word. Mind the wilds on the road — spiders in the silk hollows, bears in the briars, goblin ambushers everywhere.

Splitjaw's warband is broken and the south road is ours again. Nothing stands between us and the Temple now — and I will not lie to you. The cult there serves something that wears our own Warden like a cloak. End it, and Aldenmere is truly free.

Sorrel lives — freed after nine years in that thing's grip. The Flame has fled deep into the Lightwell, and Aldenmere's streets are ours to the last stone. Whatever you ask of this Council, it is yours.
The Council sits day and night until every quarter is reclaimed. Speak to the Exchange if you need arms.
